---
title: "Jeremías Likerman"
subtitle: "Adjunct Researcher at CONICET · Associate Professor at the University of Buenos Aires"
image: ../profile.png
about:
  template: jolla
  links:
    - icon: envelope
      text: Email
      href: mailto:jlikerman@uba.ar
    - icon: journal-text
      text: Google Scholar
      href: https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=HGA3sQQAAAAJ
    - icon: link-45deg
      text: ORCID
      href: https://orcid.org/0000-0003-3844-2085
    - icon: github
      text: GitHub
      href: https://github.com/likerman
lang: en
---

::: {.callout-note appearance="simple" icon=false}
Geoscientist specializing in **structural geology, geomechanics, and multiscale numerical modelling**. I integrate field observations, analogue experiments, and 2D/3D simulations to investigate Andean geodynamics, crustal deformation, volcanic geothermal systems, and fracture networks in energy reservoirs.
:::

[Download full CV in English](../CV_JSPS_English/Jeremias_Likerman_CV_JSPS.pdf){.btn .btn-primary} [Ver CV en español](../index.qmd){.btn .btn-outline-primary}

## Current appointments

- **Adjunct Researcher**, CONICET–IDEAN, University of Buenos Aires. CONICET researcher since 2018; promoted to Investigador Adjunto in November 2023.
- **Associate Professor**, Department of Geological Sciences, University of Buenos Aires, since June 2026. Courses: Geostatistics and Geoinformatics.
- **Institutional Coordinator at IDEAN**, since September 2025; Institute Council member since April 2024.

## Research areas

::: {.grid}
::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}
### Andean geodynamics

Two- and three-dimensional thermomechanical modelling of subduction zones, flat-slab segments, mantle–lithosphere interaction, upper-plate deformation, and mountain building.
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}
### Volcanic geothermal systems

Assessment of the geothermal potential of Copahue, Domuyo, Socompa, and Cerro Blanco using thermal, thermo-hydraulic, and structural models.
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}
### Structural geology and geomechanics

Fracture prediction, fault-propagation folding, tectonic inversion, and connectivity of natural fracture networks.
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}
### Scientific computing and HPC

Models developed with Underworld2/3, ASPECT, GOLEM, and LaMEM, including reproducible workflows and high-performance computing.
:::
:::

## Education

- **PhD in Geological Sciences**, University of Buenos Aires, 2015.
- **BSc in Geological Sciences**, University of Buenos Aires, 2010.
- **CONICET–Universitat Politècnica de Catalunya Postdoctoral Fellowship**, 2015–2017.
- **Visiting Researcher**, Stanford University, 2013.
- Research stays at UPC and GEO3BCN–CSIC.

## Selected projects

- **PIP 11220250100817CO, CONICET** — *Tectonic-structural analysis through analogue and numerical modelling: contributions to energy resources*. Co-Principal Investigator, 2026–2029.
- **Williams Foundation** — *Quaternary deformation in the Famatina System*. Co-Investigator, 2025–2026.
- **LINCGL2024** — Geothermal-potential assessment of volcanic zones using thermomechanical numerical models. Principal Investigator, 2024–2026.
- **PICT 2021-I-A-01210** — *Traverse structures in the Andean orogen*. Team member, 2023–2027.
- MareNostrum/BSC AECT projects on subduction, mantle plumes, and geothermal systems, 2022–2026.

## Selected publications

1. Villarroel, M. A. et al., including **Likerman, J.** (2026). *The role of compressional structures in the development of collapse calderas: Insights from analogue models*. **Journal of Volcanology and Geothermal Research**, 473, 108582.
2. Gianni, G. M., Gallo, L. C., **Likerman, J.**, et al. (2025). *Slab underthrusting is the primary control on flat-slab size*. **Science Advances**, 11(28).
3. Correa-Luna, C., Yagupsky, D. L., **Likerman, J.**, and Barcelona, H. (2025). *Impact of large-scale structures on fracture network connectivity: Insights into the Vaca Muerta unconventional play*. **Tectonophysics**, 230640.
4. Plotek, B., **Likerman, J.**, and Cristallini, E. (2024). *Geomechanical modeling of fault-propagation folds*. **Journal of Structural Geology**, 105064.
5. Gianni, G. M., **Likerman, J.**, Navarrete, C. R., Gianni, C. R., and Zlotnik, S. (2023). *Ghost-arc geochemical anomaly at a spreading ridge caused by supersized flat subduction*. **Nature Communications**, 14, 2083.
6. **Likerman, J.**, Zlotnik, S., and Li, C.-F. (2021). *The effects of small-scale convection in the shallow lithosphere of the North Atlantic*. **Geophysical Journal International**, 227(3), 1512–1522.

[Complete publication list on Google Scholar](https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=HGA3sQQAAAAJ){.btn .btn-outline-secondary}

## Research supervision

Supervision and co-supervision of one assistant researcher, one postdoctoral fellowship, one completed PhD, and eleven undergraduate theses in geomechanics, active tectonics, geothermal systems, and numerical modelling.

## International cooperation

Collaborations with GEO3BCN–CSIC, LaCaN–UPC, the Barcelona Supercomputing Center, GFZ Potsdam, Kobe University, and other international groups. Incoming JSPS Research Fellowship at Kobe University in 2027, hosted by Prof. Shoichi Yoshioka.

## Methods and tools

**Modelling:** Underworld2/3, ASPECT, GOLEM, LaMEM · **Programming:** Python, R, MATLAB, LaTeX · **Geospatial:** QGIS, GMT, PostGIS · **Methods:** analogue modelling, PIV, structural analysis, geological mapping, and HPC workflows.
